title: [lichen_l2.c:958-969] enable path re-init clears replay_table but peers may have valid replay state
description:
Completeness: State consistency category.

In lichen_l2_enable() enable path (line 958-969), when link_ctx is re-initialized:
1. Line 957: secure_zero(&link_ctx, ...) clears key material
2. Line 958: lichen_link_init() re-initializes link context
3. Line 969: lichen_replay_table_init() clears entire replay table

However, the peer_table is NOT cleared in the enable path (only in disable path at 1071-1075).

This creates a state inconsistency: after disable/enable cycle:
- peer_table still has peer entries (unless cleared by disable)
- replay_table is cleared
- Peers that were mid-communication will fail replay checks until their sequence numbers exceed the cleared replay window

This is arguably correct (replay protection resets = secure), but the asymmetry between peer_table preservation and replay_table clearing may be surprising.

Document this behavior or consider clearing both together for consistency.
close_reason: Added clarifying comment: replay_table cleared on enable for security, peer_table preserved to avoid EDHOC re-handshake
